analysis.mistake: FooterInfoSocialItem.tsx used __experimentalLinkControl + BlockControls + Popover for link editing, plus MediaUpload inside IconPicker for logo selection. This heavy component tree triggered core-data store operations (editEntityRecord) during the block insertion lifecycle, before the entity record was ready. The 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading meta)' console error occurred when adding new social media items, specifically during the store synchronization phase.
analysis.expected: Adding a new social media item should work without console errors on the first click. The block should be ready to accept link and icon configuration immediately after insertion.
analysis.rootCause: The heavy component tree (__experimentalLinkControl + MediaUpload) forced WordPress core-data to run editEntityRecord during block insertion, before the entity meta fields were initialized. The race condition occurred specifically in FooterInfoSocialItem's lifecycle, not in the parent Footer.tsx. Replacing the heavy components with a lightweight Link component (which doesn't trigger core-data ops) and deferring IconPicker rendering (via isSelected conditional) until user interaction eliminated these premature store operations.

suggestions.content: Defer heavy Gutenberg components (MediaUpload, __experimentalLinkControl, Popover with form controls) until block is selected (isSelected=true). Render lightweight static fallbacks during insertion/unselected state. This prevents core-data store operations from firing during block creation lifecycle, which can race with entity meta initialization and cause 'undefined' errors.
suggestions.rationale: Heavy components trigger editEntityRecord during insertion before entity is ready. Deferring to selected state allows entity registration to complete before store operations begin.

suggestions.content: For link editing in child blocks, prefer @taktdev/components Link component over @wordpress/block-editor __experimentalLinkControl. Link wraps LinkControl internally without requiring separate BlockControls, Popover, ToggleControl, or TextControl imports, reducing component tree size and eliminating unnecessary store bindings during insertion.
suggestions.rationale: Lightweight wrapper components reduce surface area for accidental store subscriptions and are safer during block lifecycle events. Link component encapsulates link UI patterns while reducing imports and cognitive overhead.
